comments.text: I have posted a preprint giving partial results at the one-third threshold and clarifying the witness-convention issue in this problem: Eric Li, "On the Duke--Erdős--Rödl Problem at the One-Third Threshold", arXiv:2606.06522. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06522 Write $\rho=e(G)/n^2$. The positive result is that if $\rho\geq n^{-1/3}$, equivalently if $e(G)\geq n^2/k$ with $k\leq n^{1/3}$, then $G$ contains subgraphs $H_6,H_8$ such that\[ e(H_6)=\Omega(\rho^3 n^2),\qquad e(H_8)=\Omega(\rho^2 n^2), \]where every two distinct edges of $H_6$ lie together on a cycle of length at most $6$ contained in $H_6$, and every two distinct edges of $H_8$ lie together on a cycle of length at most $8$ contained in $H_8$. Thus the $C_{\leq 8}$ statement is proved in the polynomial range $\rho\geq n^{-1/3}$, improving the previously recorded range $\rho>n^{-1/5}$ for that part. The same argument also gives an internal $C_{\leq 6}$ core of the expected size $\Omega(\rho^3n^2)$ in this range, but this $C_{\leq6}$ result does not impose the extra adjacent-edge $C_4$ condition from the strongest formulation of the problem. There is also an ambient strong $C_6$ companion result. If $e(G)\geq n^2/k$ with $k=o(n^{1/2})$, then $G$ contains an edge set $F$ with\[ |F|=\Omega(n^2/k^3) \]such that every two distinct edges of $F$ are witnessed by a cycle of length at most $6$ in the ambient graph $G$, and adjacent pairs are witnessed by ambient $C_4$'s. Here the witness cycles are allowed to use edges outside $F$. The preprint also gives a negative result showing that the adjacent-edge $C_4$ condition is genuinely convention-sensitive. Under the internal strong $C_6$ convention, where the witnessing cycles must lie inside the subgraph and adjacent pairs must lie on $C_4$'s inside the subgraph, the bound $\Omega(\rho^3n^2)$ cannot hold at fixed polynomial densities $\rho=\Theta(n^{-\beta})$ for $\beta\in[1/3,1/2)$. More precisely, for every fixed $\beta\in[1/3,1/2)$ there are bipartite graphs $G$ with\[ e(G)=\Theta_\beta(n^{2-\beta}),\qquad \rho(G)=\Theta_\beta(n^{-\beta}), \]such that every internally strongly $C_6$-connected subgraph $H\subseteq G$ has only\[ e(H)=O_\beta\left(\frac{\rho(G)^3n^2}{(\log n)^2}\right) \]edges. So the upshot is: 1. the $C_{\leq8}$ statement is proved up to the one-third threshold $\rho\geq n^{-1/3}$; 2. an internal $C_{\leq6}$ core of the expected size is also proved up to this threshold, but without the adjacent-edge $C_4$ requirement; 3. the internal strong $C_6$ version with adjacent-edge $C_4$ witnesses has a random-lift obstruction starting at the same one-third threshold.
Looks like GPT-5.4 Pro can construct a counterexample to the second statement if there's no additional conditions on sparsity. Have a look here for discussion and references.
Thanks! I ran standard check which claimed 2 minor issues, both in Proposition 4 which did not affect Theorem 1 which proved the main counterexample.
